#Denali2022 #TheColdOne #NineDaysOfJoy!
It's been 3 years since the last attempt and it felt like a good time to get back out there again! Me and Aurimas recruited #MasterChef Vytas and #MasterSherpa Laurynas to the crew - nothing can stop us now! 🙂
Day 1. BC to C1 (2200m - 2300m). A small plane dropped us off in Kahiltna BC at mid-day. As pretty as we left it 3 years ago! Roped up and started walking to C1. #HereWeGoAgain!! The rangers advised walking at night due to crevasse danger. However, we are on our own "efficient" schedule and need to push. The loads are heavy and you can sense right away that some more training at home would not have hurt 😛 4h and we are at Ski Hill. Done for the day? Hearts says "no", brain says "yes", coz it's freezing cold and I can't remember if there's any natural protection from the wind further up. #MiniPanicMoment - even though we triple checked the gear list, we forgot tent stakes for one of the tents… first thought: "is this attempt over???" #KeepCalm. Nah, we'll find a solution - build higher walls and improvise with other gear. Happy that we stopped for the day :))
Day 2. Ski Hill to C3 (2300m-3400m). Feeling of "I know this part, let's get it over with". 1100m of vertical ascent in a single carry with 45-50kg load. It's a physically tough day and we started to get worn down pretty quickly. The scorching sun did not help #MiamiBeach. #ExtremelySurprised how much the glacier, the surrounding mountains and "the path" has changed in 3 years - can't recognize anything! #SlowlySlowly and we are in C3! Around 70 tents. Another long evening doing house chores - building snow walls and melting snow. Bpm 97 while resting. Probably overdid it, but Pringles always helps to recover! 😛 (I initially asked the team to buy 16 packs for the expedition but got overruled and settled for 8 only) 😛
Day 3. C3 to C4 (3400m - 4300m). As per our already "efficient" schedule, we had to do a cache (leave half of the gear up) and come back to C3. But the weather forecast had other plans - so we packed everything up and decided to do a single carry to C4. Shaving off one day of the "efficient" schedule and also one day less compared to 3 years ago.. the day that was one of the toughest.. #Ugh.. "Are we making the same mistake again??". It took time to take the decision and pack up - so we left only at 11am and agreed to cache stuff on the way in case needed. #SlowlySlowly. The conditions kept changing - hot, cold, windy, icy - and we continued to adjust. Taking the time, resting. #NoRush. Reached the Windy Corner (which was not windy this time, but still slippery and full of crevasses underneath), walked over a few mini-crevasses and it was time for a cache at 4100m. However, everyone preferred to continue with the single carry. I felt the same - spending a day chilling sounded vastly better than walking back here and up tomorrow 🙂 the last meters were brutal; we gave all that we had left and somehow arrived to C4! The brain is off, the head hurts, the body is done - can't take any decisions. Crap, only 1h till the sunset - need to set up the tents and melt some snow for the night. We set up "make-shift" tents next to Moldovans who planned to move up the next day - we'd take over their spots. (Fun fact, it was Day #9 for them already and Day #3 for us) 🙂 no energy left whatsoever - could not even spare a few minutes to say 'hi' to Estonians who were camping some 200m away. #GottaSleep! #OverAndOut!
Day 4. Rest day. #Finally. It's super cold in C4 when the sun is gone and baking hot when the sun comes back! 🙂 Took over the spot from Moldovans and went for a short walk up to "collect some altitude meters". Hiked up some 250m and admired the views of C4. #Fantastic! As sunny as it gets! Met some extremely happy people coming down from their successful summits 🙂 #FingersCrossed. Also, it seemed like a busy day for the helicopter - collected a few folks from the ridge. #Gossips. Met Estonians - Day #9 for them also and they plan to use the next 2-3 days for summitting - #GoodLuck! #Damn, it's super difficult to let the weather window pass by - even though we are not ready yet and it would be extremely tough if we attempted to join the crowd. While the heart says "go for it - this might be the only chance", the brain says "you'll have to sit this one out". 
Day 5. C4 - Ridge - C4. (4300m - 4900m - 4300m). Woke up at 7am - a few hours before the sunrise. #FreakingCold! Need to melt a ton of snow and it just takes forever.. we could see long queues of people forming next to the fixed lines as everyone's going for it. I remind myself "we are not well rested & ready yet". Not in a rush today, so we leave only at 10:30 and take our time. Some 20kg on the back is taking its toll. #TooMuchStuff. Crevasses, blue ice and steep terrain does not help. Took 4.5h to get to the top of the ridge. Cached above the ridge, not too deep - it's hard to cut into ice at that altitude. Hope that some wild birds don't dig it up or that the sun does not melt too much snow 🙂 wtf, some Kyrgyz person nearly slided down the ridge in front of our eyes chasing after his thermos. #WhatADumbIdea. #SlowlySlowly, we head back down to C4 ('the scorching oven') - no clouds again. The weather forecast worsened for the upcoming days/week, so we might be in for a long one this time..
Day 6. Rest day. Whoever you talk to, everyone seems to have their own weather forecast. Our own forecast significantly changes every few hours.. no idea whom to trust :)) let's wait for the evening forecast. We brought some salami, cheese, bread and ketchup up to C4. #SalamiTactics. It's a very delicious treat but also 'very heavy' (compared to barely edible freeze-dried food that weights nothing)! 🙂 Saved some of the treat for the special occasion - the evening before we decide to go for the summit, so that we get extra energy and motivation! 🙂 evening forecast comes in & it's hammer time.. luckily, the weather window seems to have extended and we may have a chance! However, we'd have to skip one acclimatization/resting day at C5.. Salamis on the table help to take the decision quicker - let's have the feast and let's go for it! 🙂 #LetsDoThis! #NoWayBack!
We heard the news that #EstoniansSummitted! #Congratz! Moldovans are back to C4 - they summitted as well! 🙂
Day 7. C4 - C5 (4300m-5200m). We were not the only ones with the same plans. It seemed like half of the Camp 4 decided to move up. Took some 3h to get drinking water ready and left at around 9am. No sun yet, so it's freezing cold. We are quick today - 3.5h and we are above the ridge! #SalamiMotivation! Dug up our cache and took 1h to stuff it in (windy on the ridge). #NoRisks. The bags are freaking heavy, can barely lift mine! #ReallyReallyHeavy. #BackBreakingHeavy. Ugh, 200m of vertical ascent.. i.e. half a day walking on the ridge around big boulders and climbing up with this.. #NotSoSure. #SlowlySlowly made it to C5 - so little energy left, need to set up a tent. Wherever we go, some #AmericanGuidingTeam 'claims' half of the C5 as 'theirs' (i.e. in essence, everything that has any protection built up). What a terrible mountaineering example. We even let these teams pass by us on the ridge and that's how they pay off. They are some 50 people, we are 4. Anyway, we'll just have to use some of the energy we don't really have to build our own protections. We are at 5200m and every move takes its toll. Took us 4h to build it up and get drinking water. Luckily there's little wind, so average walls will do. Extremely tired, but excited about tomorrow! Btw, the ridge views were fantastic - no real need of going any further 😛
Day 8. Summit day! (5200m - 6195m - 5200m). Yey!! The day I've been waiting for 3 years!! Woke up at 6am, melted snow and left before 10am. (Too cold to leave earlier). We were one of the first teams to start - we knew that the day was long for us. #SlowlySlowly, #NotInAHurry. No need to break the world record, just a simple summit is enough! 🙂 we let a few quicker teams pass by, chatted with some others when taking a break. The only annoying people on the mountain were again American guiding teams - no respect for others, the mountain belongs to them, move away! And they pushed their clients quite aggressively - no time to chill, only rushing up! The climb to the summit is super long - when you reach a certain milestone, it takes another hour or two to get to the next one. And it is always up. Always freaking up.. (There’s no way I could have done this roundtrip 3 years ago in 8h). #Rest #Eat #Drink #Up #Repeat. One milestone after another.. I am so exhausted. Here's the autobahn, Denali pass, Zebra rocks, the football field and then.. only a few hundred meters up to the summit ridge?? Nearly there?? Freaking steep! Step-by-step, another break before the final push. #EveryStepBringsYouCloser! The summit ridge is not for the faint hearted - massive drops on both sides. One wrong step and you might drag your whole team down the mountain. We are lucky because there's no wind and the view is super clear! #Wow! We are here!! Here's the summit marker!!! Yeyyy!!!! We did it!!! Can't believe it, it's DENALI SUMMIT!!
(I think I was super happy because I knew I would not need to attempt the mountain and go through all this suffering yet again!! 🙂 ) spent some 10min on the top taking pics and enjoying the feeling! As clear as it gets! Still, it gets pretty cold fairly quickly - so cold that GoPro gave up on me on the way to the summit 🙂
On the way back we got a reminder that the mountaineering risks are real - there was a helicopter hovering around at the time we summitted. We soon learned that a 45-year-old with one of the American guiding teams suffered a fatal heart attack some 100m below the summit. Can't really do much about it at this altitude. By the time we passed by, he was already gone, and a few guides were preparing him for an airlift. To put it into perspective, there were some 100 people attempting to summit on this day.
That reminded us to be careful and not overdo it ourselves. Going back to C5 felt like forever, but we kept securing ourselves wherever we could. Stopping and taking breaks whenever anyone found it difficult. We saw some other folks barely standing being helped/walked down by their guides. #ToughTough. After nearly 14 gruelling hours we were back in C5! Just right before the sunset and before it gets really cold! We did it!! A few litres of water to melt and off to get some proper sleep! 🙂 #Happy #Emotional #GreatSuccess!
Day 9. C5 to BC (5200m - 2200m). When I went to sleep after the summit, I thought of not moving the next day at all 🙂 but Vytas had other plans - it made sense to get off the mountain asap, because the weather window was closing and some bad weather was coming. So we packed everything up (felt even heavier!), melted some snow and started heading down. The lower we got, the more oxy we had and the better we felt. The closer we were to the goal, the more motivation we had to keep moving! 🙂 #SlowlySlowly, C5 --> C4.. C4 --> C3.. surprisingly, we met Estonians in C3 resting and melting snow! 🙂 even though we did not talk about it, the race to the BC was on! Well, at least in our heads :)) we were around 12 hours on the road when we reached Ski Hill. Tired and exhausted. Decided to sleep for a few hours and continue during the night to BC - due to lower risk of falling into a crevasse, because we truly wanted to get off the mountain and because we had beers cached at the base camp! 🙂 #hehe! #MotivationMatters! We were at BC at 7:30, just before the 'camp operator' started her day. Around 100 people were already waiting for a lift off on this day. Estonians arrived and joined the queue as well. While we were enjoying our beers, an avalanche struck nearby. At first it seemed #Wow! But as the resulting snowstorm was getting closer to us and nobody knew what to do - some started running & hiding, some did nothing. Luckily, we just got covered in snow and nothing bad happened. #Enough! Time to fly out!  #FasterFaster
When you are all battered, wet and nearly shaking from exhaustion, you really want to get out asap.. due to some lucky coincidence, the first plane that landed happened to be ours! 🙂 We were the first team on their list to be flown off the mountain! Truly jealous for ourselves 😛 Civilization, here we come!! #Hooray! #CouldntBeHappier! What an amazing feeling! 🙂
#ByeByeDenali! #SeeYouNeverAgain! 🙂
